title: begonia × lapeyrousei
intro: Begonia × lapeyrousei, also known as Begonia lapeyrousei, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Begoniaceae. It is primarily used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Begonia × lapeyrousei is often used as a tea or infused in oils to extract its medicinal compounds, although its uses are largely anecdotal and further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
